decision_policy.adoption_style: adopts when the payoff is concrete and the risk feels bounded
rejection_triggers: another tool that adds one more dashboard without replacing existing work
setup that depends on perfect habits from day one
proof_requirements: buy-in material that can be shared with other decision-makers
evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison
decision_policy.default_posture: Interested but skeptical; adoption happens when payoff is concrete and risk feels bounded.
evaluation_sequence: First asks whether the tool replaces an existing step or just adds another place to check
Then looks for proof from similar users and a believable before-and-after workflow
Then tests whether setup is low friction enough to survive a busy week
Finally considers whether manager approval and internal comparison can be cleared without excessive effort
minimum_to_buy: evidence of time saved in ordinary messy workflows
confidence that the tool can survive internal review and comparison
approval_constraints: decision process is slowed by committee-style comparison
switching cost is high because existing habits and records are spread across familiar tools

interview_behavior: answers best when prompted with concrete scenarios
tends to compare a new idea against current workarounds rather than discuss it abstractly
will volunteer friction points once trust is established, especially around setup and follow-through
likely_phrases: Show me how this fits into what I already do.
I do not need another place to check.
If this saves time, it has to save time in real cases, not just the clean version.
I would need something I can show my manager, not just a pitch.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: {"behavioral_realism": "The enrichment focuses on routine pressure, scattered context, approval friction, and tool fatigue because those are credible consequences of the fixed anchors: account manager role, tool-heavy workflow, medium schedule pressure, high switching cost, and peer-proof requirements.", "fixed_anchor_preservation": "Identity, locale, household structure, occupation, life stage, language, and economic constraints remain consistent with the provided anchors. The persona is expanded through trade-offs and workflow logic rather than changed demographic facts.", "guardrails": "The profile avoids medical, legal, or diagnostic claims and does not treat non-binary identity, age, or location as deterministic behavior. Sensitive context is handled as boundaries and lived considerations, not stereotypes.", "product_validation_use": "This persona is tuned to evaluate onboarding trust, workflow replacement value, privacy boundaries, and proof requirements, which makes it useful for testing discovery, concept evaluation, and prototype validation."}
